mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns (win rate < 50%), or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During high-inflation regimes, unexpected CPIAUCSL increases force immediate equity repricing as investors anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.3% MoM during high-inflation periods, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in inflationary environments.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which directly supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth correlates with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 150 thousand confirm robust economic momentum, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 60-day returns in > 60% of months where PAYEMS growth surpasses 150k, or the threshold signal shows no out-of-sample predictive accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and rising unemployment creates stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations and reduces earnings growth, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high FEDFUNDS and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day returns (win rate < 45%), or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms economic stabilization, which reduces tail-risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% during recovery phases, or the signal loses predictive power outside recovery periods.
